vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Tool flange with standard bolt pattern and locating boss
Pneumatic locking mechanism
Four utility ports
CNC machining + LPBF manufacturing strategy
Primary locating faces visible and labeled
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces with clearances
At least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Standard fit vocabulary for all mating interfaces
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum visible
Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels
Joint cartridges removable without cutting printed links
Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range
Mass estimation within 200g budget

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on harmonic drive due to heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling system or increase cooling fin surface area.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to inadequate strain relief.
risk.mit: Add strain relief clamps and ensure cables follow recommended bend radius.

risk.desc: Insufficient accessibility for electronics module replacement.
risk.mit: Design larger access panels or improve visibility of module location.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment of mounting holes affecting structural integrity.
risk.mit: Increase tolerance standards for manufacturing and assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of harmonic drive failure due to over-torque.
risk.mit: Include torque limiters or protective mechanisms in the design.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

integration_opportunities.description: The electronics module is designed to be easily replaceable for maintenance or upgrade purposes.
The cable entry grommet ensures proper cable management and strain relief.

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC machining for base plate and mounting holes, LPBF for complex internal components like harmonic drive and cooling fins.
manufacturing_strategy.datum: Base plate center and mounting hole pattern
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.1mm for machined parts, ±0.2mm for printed components
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for easy removal during post-processing
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection of machined surfaces, non-destructive testing (NDT) for printed components

verification_focus.description: Ensure all mounting holes are accurately positioned for secure attachment to the robot base frame.
Verify the harmonic drive can achieve the specified torque and rotation range.
Confirm the electronics module is properly connected and accessible for maintenance.

generated_parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
generated_parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

requirements: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075
Vehicle context: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach
Discipline: Structures - Role: Rigid links with load path from tool flange to base, ribs where needed
Evidence features: link_body, rib, base_flange
Part consolidation with guardrails: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible
Fastener joint sanity: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented
Inspection readability: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON
Fastener locking and preload evidence: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction
Thread and insert engagement sanity: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole
Axis datum readability: J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible
Cable spine serviceability: Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels
Joint cartridge replaceability: Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links
Bearing shaft mounting sanity: Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent for inner/outer races
Moving joint harness workmanship: Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range

verify.rule: All required fields are present.

All required features are represented in geometry.
Interfaces are reciprocal with adjacent modules.
Some fastener patterns lack explicit locking and preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to aluminum usage.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient stiffness leading to deformation under load.

risk.mit: Optimize cable channel layout and ensure sufficient clearance.

risk.mit: Increase size of access windows and ensure clear labeling.

integration_opportunities.description: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
J1-J6 axis rings, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible.
Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels.
Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links.
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range.
